title: [HXC-DISC] Resolve the production Haxe carrier for C semantics
description:
Context: Pinned Haxe 4.3.7 uses its Cross platform when Reflaxe runs without a native Haxe output target. Cross irreversibly initializes target.name=cross, target.utf16/utf16, target.sys, static, and related platform facts before initialization macros. The public macro API can add or overwrite values but cannot remove a define, so relabeling Cross as C would type UTF-16 stdlib and application branches contrary to ADR 0004/0007.

Scope: Choose and implement a production Haxe carrier whose compiler configuration matches reflaxe.c semantics, or explicitly supersede the affected owner decisions with measured compatibility consequences. Evaluate at least an exact Haxe 4 compiler patch/fork, a Haxe version with supported custom targets, and a documented Cross-plus-overrides strategy.

Non-scope: Implement the C String runtime, broad stdlib parity, or relax the UTF-8 scalar contract silently.

Validation: Run an actual non-probe reflaxe.c typing invocation and structural stdlib report. Prove static C-appropriate typing, target.name=c, c, target.unicode, absence of target.utf16/utf16, no unrelated carrier conditional, and adapter-derived sys/thread/atomic capabilities under cold and compiler-server builds.

Compatibility: Preserve ordinary Haxe conditionals and fail closed until the selected carrier is reproducible on every supported development host. Any compiler fork or version move must update the immutable toolchain/provenance contract.

Documentation: Record the accepted alternative in ADR 0007 or a superseding ADR and reconcile toolchain, configuration, architecture, PRD, AGENTS, and downstream Beads evidence.

## Rationale

Compiler platform configuration controls nullability, scoping, capture, exceptions, standard-library precedence, Unicode units, and capability conditionals before Reflaxe lowering. A target-name-only rewrite would make reports look correct while preserving contradictory typed branches, so production bootstrap cannot proceed without an explicit carrier decision and executable evidence.

## Alternatives Considered

- Build and provenance-pin an exact Haxe 4.3.7 compiler patch that adds a C platform configuration. This could preserve the language pin but creates cross-platform compiler distribution and maintenance obligations.
- Move to a Haxe version with supported custom-target configuration. This could provide the cleanest upstream API but requires compatibility review of Haxe/Reflaxe/compiler behavior and a new immutable pin.
- Keep Haxe 4 Cross and override affected standard-library modules/conditionals. This minimizes compiler distribution work but cannot hide global cross/UTF-16 facts from arbitrary portable application source and is therefore not acceptable without a superseding compatibility decision.
- Use Eval or another built-in target as the production carrier. Current evidence rejects this as a silent import of unrelated type-system, stdlib, and capability semantics; Eval remains a test oracle only.
acceptance_criteria: An accepted decision names the production carrier and rejected alternatives; the exact pinned toolchain supplies a static C-appropriate platform configuration; actual target typing exposes target.name=c, c, and target.unicode without target.utf16/utf16 or an unrelated carrier conditional; sys/thread/atomic facts come only from the resolved adapter; upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString branch snapshots and cold/server probes pass; E0.T04 remains blocked until this evidence is executable.
close_reason: Accepted exact official Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 at revision 2c1e544e0a2c7524ef4c8e103f1b0580362ea538 as the production typing carrier. Direct builds use --custom-target c=<output>; automatic c.Init.init()/TargetPlatform configure a static, block-scoped, scalar-Unicode PlatformConfig before typing, derive sys only for hosted, and advertise no threads/atomics without adapter evidence. CompilerInit validates that snapshot and derives Reflaxe c_output from Compiler.getOutput(), rejecting missing or mismatched output. Actual non-probe registration reaches source-root HXC1000 and emits no artifact in cold and compiler-server builds; lifecycle, staged-package, non-C isolation, recovery, hosted/freestanding, and upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString snapshots pass. ADR 0007, ADR 0004, PRD, README, AGENTS, architecture, configuration, toolchain, provenance, research, and checked plan now agree. Rejected Haxe 4 Cross because unremovable UTF-16 facts leak to application conditionals; rejected a Haxe 4 fork because of cross-host distribution/maintenance burden; retained Eval only for future hxc bootstrap, differential oracle, and isolation. Exact official platform artifact hashes are lock-checked. Evidence: npm test; npm run deps:verify; jq empty docs/specs/*.json; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ci/check_toolchain.py test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; bd lint --json (0); bd dep cycles --json ([]); git diff --check.

title: [HXC-DISC] Validate C target identity and Unicode platform defines
description:
Context: ADR 0007 chooses c_output as the Haxe 4/Reflaxe activation signal, c/target.name=c as public identity, target.unicode without target.utf16, and capability-derived platform defines. The partial checkout cannot yet prove that these signals activate exactly once or select the intended Haxe 4.3.7 stdlib branches.

Scope: Under the pinned Haxe/Reflaxe toolchain, add focused macro/type-check probes for c_output and target.name activation, non-C no-op behavior, public/internal define visibility, Unicode/UTF-16 selection, and conflicting platform definitions. Record the resolved compiler configuration and typed upstream String/StringTools branches.

Non-scope: Implement the C string runtime, Unicode algorithms, or broad stdlib parity; E4.T03/E5 own those.

Validation: Positive and negative HXML/macro fixtures show exactly-once activation, c public identity, reflaxe_c implementation-only guidance, target.unicode=true, target.utf16=false, and source-positioned rejection of conflicting identities/capabilities. The pinned target-owned Haxe bootstrap type-check gate runs the probes reproducibly.

Compatibility: Ordinary Haxe conditionals use #if c; c_output stays a path-bearing build transport. Capability flags are never asserted merely because the target is C.

Documentation: Reconcile ADR 0007, configuration/agent guidance, and E0.T03/E0.T04 evidence if implementation constraints require a superseding decision.
acceptance_criteria: c_output and target.name=c activation probes initialize the compiler exactly once; non-C builds remain untouched; c and Unicode platform conditions select the documented Haxe 4.3.7 branches; conflicting identity/UTF-16/capability inputs fail at a source position; the result is integrated into the reproducible bootstrap type-check gate.
notes:
Created from E0.T01 as the deferred experiment required before bootstrap hardens ADR 0007. It blocks E0.T04 and is blocked by E0.T03; it does not own String runtime semantics or broad stdlib work.
E0.T03 established the lifecycle subset under the exact pin: cold and compiler-server probes cover c_output activation, non-C isolation, exactly-once registration guards, public c, implementation-only reflaxe_c, target.unicode, and absence/rejection of target.utf16. This discovery remains open for target.name/custom-target activation, conflicting identity and capability inputs, source-positioned diagnostics, and proof of the selected upstream Haxe stdlib branches.
Resolved evidence: CompilerInit now normalizes target.name=c only after carrier validation, records exactly-once bootstrap/init counts and the raw carrier, adds root-anchored HXC0003 conflicts, and never treats caller -D c as activation. test/bootstrap/expected/target-contract.json proves c_output and compiler-owned target.name activation, public c versus internal reflaxe_c, target.unicode without target.utf16/utf16, and the scalar typed fields from pinned upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString under the explicit Eval lifecycle carrier. Negative probes cover reserved target.* CLI input, unrelated identity/carrier, UTF-16, atomics, freestanding capability leakage, and the actual Haxe 4 Cross carrier. Cross predefines unremovable UTF-16 facts, so production bootstrap remains fail-closed and decision haxe_c-od2.6 now blocks E0.T04 and E4.T03.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-003, HXC-SCAF-007, and HXC-SEM-006 target-identity validation. CompilerBootstrap/CompilerInit now expose deterministic count=1 markers, validate the raw Haxe carrier before normalizing target.name=c, preserve c_output as transport and c as the public conditional, retain reflaxe_c as implementation-only, add target.unicode without adding capability flags, and issue HXC0003 at the compilation root for conflicting identity, UTF-16, unproven atomics, or freestanding carrier facts. test/bootstrap/TargetContractProbe.hx plus expected/target-contract.json structurally prove the pinned upstream String.hx, StringTools.hx, and UnicodeString.hx scalar branches, target.utf16=false/utf16=false, target.name=c, public/internal visibility, c_output and compiler-owned target.name activation, caller -D c non-activation, non-C isolation, duplicate idempotence, package/CWD behavior, and valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation. The experiment also proved a material pin constraint: real Haxe 4.3.7 Cross predefines target.name=cross and unremovable target.utf16/utf16 before macros. Production Cross now fails closed instead of typing the wrong program; new P0 decision haxe_c-od2.6 evaluates a pinned Haxe 4 patch, a supported custom-target Haxe version, or a superseding compatibility choice and blocks E0.T04/E4.T03. ADR 0004/0007, PRD, architecture, configuration, toolchain, research, README, AGENTS, diagnostic catalog, CI, pre-commit, and live downstream notes agree that Eval is a test carrier only and its sys/threaded facts are not C capability claims. Evidence: npm test; npm run test:bootstrap after final formatting; jq empty docs/specs/*.json test/bootstrap/expected/target-contract.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; git diff --check.

title: [HXC-DISC] Restore typed C authoring surface and macro contract
description:
Context: The partial checkout lacks the target-facing C abstractions named by the PRD, while the product contract requires C developers to author headers, declarations, layout, linkage, ownership, and other C-native facts through typed Haxe surfaces.

Scope: Ratify the Haxe-first C authoring and DSL-admission contract; restore the minimal compilable c.* types, metadata, and macro seeds needed by E0.T04; and map deeper semantics to E3, E6, E7, E8, and E9 owners.

Non-scope: Complete bindgen, full ABI export, every pointer operation, or application DSL implementations. Those remain with their existing epics.

Validation: The pinned Haxe toolchain type-checks macro and runtime branches; positive and negative fixtures prove source-positioned validation; generated declarations remain structural and deterministic; runtime reports prove no hidden hxrt use.

Compatibility: Ordinary Haxe remains portable. c.* imports and C metadata are explicit target boundaries. Public header and ABI spellings remain provisional until the owning ADR and fixtures accept them.

Documentation: Update ADR 0002, PRD Section 17, architecture/configuration guidance, diagnostics, and the dependent Beads acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: Document justified Haxe equivalents for C declarations, header grouping, includes, qualifiers, layout, linkage, calling conventions, compile-time constants/assertions, and build facts; define an admission gate that requires typed inputs and outputs, source-positioned diagnostics, deterministic expansion evidence, explicit unsafe/runtime effects, and a reason each macro or DSL exists; restore a minimal c.* scaffold that E0.T04 can type-check; add or update downstream issue ownership without normalizing raw C injection.
notes: Ratified M0 surface: method-free compiler contracts under std/c; canonical namespaced @:c.* metadata; per-compilation TypedCContractSnapshot with forward/complete dependencies, deduplicated build facts, and explicit empty runtime effects; c.StaticAssert.require admitted; c.Syntax/c.Unsafe remain non-operational. Downstream ownership notes updated on E0.T04, E0.T06, E1.T01/T03/T04, E3.T01, E6.T03/T04/T05/T09, E7.T01/T02/T03, E8.T04/T07, E9.T06/T08, and E10.T09.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for the M0 contract slice of HXC-MAC-001, HXC-MAC-002, HXC-MAC-003, HXC-MAC-007, HXC-MAC-010, HXC-MAC-011, and HXC-MAC-012. Added 42 method-free std/c compiler contracts; canonical namespaced @:c.* metadata; per-compilation TypedCContractSnapshot/TypedCContractMacro with structural type refs, forward/complete declaration edges, normalized/deduplicated build facts, explicit effects, and HXC5002 validation; admitted compile-time-only c.StaticAssert.require while c.Syntax/c.Unsafe remain non-operational. docs/typed-c-authoring.md, ADR 0002, PRD §17/§32, architecture, configuration, research, README, AGENTS, durable Beads vocabulary, and downstream live issue notes now agree. Positive snapshot plus 11 negative fixtures prove typed enum/literal admission, paths/build names, reserved/duplicate symbols, pack shape, opaque by-value rejection, cycle rejection, missing args, static assertions, root-order determinism, valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation, and empty allocation/unsafe/runtime effects. Evidence: npm test; jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/typed_c/run.py test/bootstrap/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh and --staged; git diff --check; targeted trailing-whitespace scan. Actual c.* lowering, generated headers/native assertions, Clang ABI authority, public ABI stability, hxc inspection, and whole-program runtime plans remain with the documented E0/E1/E3/E4/E6/E7/E8/E9/E10 owners.

title: [HXC-DISC] Create private GitHub repository and truthful README
description:
Requirement IDs: HXC-SCAF-001, HXC-SCAF-006
PRD sections: §22, §29, §32, §36

Context: The owner requested an initial private GitHub home for the reflaxe.c scaffold and a basic compelling README.

Scope: Create fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c as a private repository with gh; set the local origin; revise README.md to communicate the vision, current M0 status, core contracts, and honest next steps; commit and push the existing in-scope scaffold.

Non-scope: Ratifying the final public license, claiming unsupported compiler capabilities, implementing compiler/runtime features, or opening a pull request.

Validation: Verify repository visibility and default branch through gh; verify README links and claims against the checkout; run available scaffold gates; push Git and Beads state when the configured remotes support them.

Compatibility: Documentation and repository hosting only; no generated-C, runtime, source-language, or public-ABI behavior changes.

Documentation: README.md and remote repository metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- GitHub repository fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c exists and remains private until the owner explicitly authorizes public visibility.
- origin points to the repository and the local default branch is pushed.
- README.md is concise, compelling, explicit that this is an M0 scaffold, and accurately explains optional hxc ergonomics plus Eval/native bootstrapping.
- Canonical GPL-3.0-only text is checked in and README/PRD license language agrees.
- A tracked, installable pre-commit chain exports Beads state, rejects staged secrets/local paths/whitespace, formats staged Haxe, and runs relevant scaffold checks without disabling other Beads hooks.
- Existing scaffold and Beads state are committed without fabricating absent capabilities.
- Validation and publication evidence are recorded in the close reason.
notes:
Owner decision: retain hxc as a later optional ergonomics/orchestration layer for C-specific native toolchain, bindgen, export, doctor, and ABI inspection workflows. Direct Haxe/HXML compilation remains canonical; hxc is not a Reflaxe requirement and must not become an opaque custom build system. Document this distinction in README.md and PRD Section 18.
Publication-readiness scope added by owner: keep GitHub visibility private for now; add GPL-3.0-only; port the proven sibling pre-commit security/path/formatting/Beads pattern with checks grounded in this scaffold.
close_reason: Requirements HXC-SCAF-001 and HXC-SCAF-006 satisfied. Artifacts: private https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c on main; README.md; LICENSE (GPL-3.0-only); .gitleaks.toml; scripts/hooks/install.sh; scripts/hooks/pre-commit; scripts/lint/local_path_guard_staged.sh; scripts/security/run-gitleaks.sh; chained .beads/hooks/pre-commit; docs/PRD.md Section 18 and architecture CLI bootstrap contract; deferred dogfood issue haxe_c-lxi.1. Publication commits: f476a72, eb5d85f, ac646eb. Validation: gh repo view confirmed PRIVATE visibility, main default branch, and GNU GPLv3 license detection; scripts/hooks/install.sh succeeded with core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks; .beads/hooks/pre-commit passed end to end; staged and history scripts/security/run-gitleaks.sh scans reported no leaks; bash -n and sh -n passed; git diff --cached --check passed; jq empty passed for all current JSON specs; strict C11 and C++17 public-header checks passed; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero findings; bd orphans --json returned null; Git main and Beads refs/dolt/data were both published. Repository remains private pending explicit owner authorization to make it public.

title: [HXC-DISC] Ratify generated-output and runtime redistribution license treatment
description:
## Decision needed

Decide and document the licensing treatment for generated C sources and headers, selectively emitted hxrt source/binaries, compiler-provided templates, generated manifests, and user-authored inputs. Keep source publication separate from package/release claims and obtain owner/legal review before promising an exception or permissive runtime grant.

## Rationale

The repository GPL-3.0-only license governs repository-owned source but does not, by itself, settle whether every compiler output is a derivative work or what terms should accompany copied runtime/template material. Users need a deliberate, consistent answer before release packaging, and the answer affects emitted SPDX data, notices, templates, and runtime selection.

## Alternatives Considered

- Assume all generated artifacts inherit GPL-3.0-only. Rejected as an unsupported blanket inference that could unnecessarily constrain users.
- Promise permissive or exception-based treatment immediately. Rejected because the exact runtime/template boundaries and owner-approved terms have not been reviewed.
- Leave the question undocumented until release. Rejected because implementation could harden conflicting output and packaging behavior.
- Track an explicit pre-release decision with runtime-free and runtime-linked fixtures. Selected as the fail-closed path.
acceptance_criteria: An accepted decision names the license/SPDX treatment for generated C and headers, generated manifests, bundled hxrt source and binaries, templates, and copied third-party inputs; public docs explain user obligations without implying legal advice; release packaging emits the decided notices and SPDX/provenance metadata; representative runtime-free and runtime-linked package fixtures pass the license policy validator.

title: [HXC-DISC] Bootstrap hxc on Eval and dogfood a native C artifact
description:
Requirement IDs: HXC-CLI-002, HXC-CLI-003, HXC-CLI-010
PRD sections: §18, §25 M8

Context: The owner ratified an Eval-first bootstrap for the optional hxc ergonomics layer, followed by dogfooding the same CLI core through reflaxe.c into a native C-built executable.

Scope: Build one target-neutral Haxe CLI core; expose it initially through haxe --run / haxelib run on Eval; add narrow host-service seams; compile the same core through reflaxe.c once required sys/runtime semantics are supported; keep direct Haxe/HXML plus a native C compiler sufficient to bootstrap releases.

Non-scope: Reimplementing the Haxe compiler, requiring native hxc to build itself, maintaining divergent Eval/native command implementations, or choosing Neko/HashLink as an additional mandatory runtime.

Validation: Run identical CLI fixtures under Eval and the generated native executable; compare exit codes, stdout/stderr, JSON, normalized build plans, and failure behavior; prove clean bootstrap without a preinstalled hxc.

Compatibility: Eval remains the development/recovery implementation and differential oracle. Native hxc is a packaged dogfood artifact, not the authoritative compiler frontend.

Documentation: README, PRD §18, installation/release documentation, and E8 packaging evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- haxe --run / haxelib run executes the shared CLI core on Eval with no extra host runtime.
- Direct Haxe/HXML plus the selected C compiler can build native hxc without an existing native hxc.
- Eval and native fixtures agree on parsing, configuration, plans, diagnostics, JSON, stdout/stderr, and exit status.
- Native packaging uses generated C from reflaxe.c and records runtime features/toolchain inputs.
- Eval remains documented and tested as a bootstrap/recovery path; no circular self-host dependency exists.

title: [HXC-E4-T03] Implement string runtime and encoding contract
description:
Stable plan key: E4.T03
Requirement IDs: HXC-PROD-005, HXC-SEM-006, HXC-RT-008
PRD sections: §12.3, §13, ADR 0004

Context: Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.

Scope: Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E4.T02, E0.T01

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Embedded NUL and invalid encoding policy are tested.
- Borrowed CString lifetime and owned conversion are explicit.
- Common literal/concat/compare paths are direct and allocation-aware.
- String feature can link without object/GC/reflection when possible.
notes:
ADR 0004 is accepted and normative. Implement private immutable valid UTF-8 storage; every Haxe index is a Unicode-scalar index; no implicit normalization; malformed UTF-8 lossy decode replaces each maximal ill-formed subsequence with U+FFFD and checked decode returns an explicit result. Bytes stays binary. Safe CString conversion rejects embedded NUL and distinguishes source-lifetime borrow from allocator-owned conversion. String must remain independently linkable from object/GC/reflection. The E0 Eval probe observed astral length/code 1/128512, composed/decomposed lengths 1/2, and embedded-NUL length 3.
Terminology clarification: ADR 0004's exact lossy policy is one U+FFFD per maximal subpart of an ill-formed UTF-8 byte sequence, not one replacement for an arbitrarily long malformed run.
Production String work is blocked by target-carrier decision haxe_c-od2.6. Haxe 4.3.7 Cross selects target.utf16/utf16 before initialization macros and cannot be relabeled safely; do not implement scalar UTF-8 semantics behind a compiler configuration that types UTF-16 branches. Reuse the structural String/StringTools/UnicodeString snapshot from haxe_c-od2.4 once the carrier is resolved.
Carrier decision haxe_c-od2.6 is resolved: the exact Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 CustomTarget(c) production snapshot selects scalar String/StringTools/UnicodeString branches with target.unicode and without target.utf16/utf16. Hosted/freestanding capabilities are target-owned; Eval remains only an independent oracle. String implementation may proceed when its planned dependencies are ready.

- Nonescaping closure/object benchmark performs zero heap allocations.
- Optimization never changes public ABI artifacts.
